inputs.label: Asset Cost
inputs.businessContext: Initial cost of the asset. Based on accounting standards (IAS 16, GAAP).

inputs.label: Salvage Value
inputs.businessContext: Estimated residual value at end of useful life. Per IAS 16.

inputs.label: Useful Life
inputs.businessContext: Expected operational life. Industry standards vary; typical range 3-30 years.

inputs.label: Depreciation Method
inputs.businessContext: Common methods per GAAP and IFRS. Straight-line is simplest; declining-balance accelerates expense.

inputs.label: Declining Balance Factor
inputs.businessContext: Factor for declining balance method (e.g., 2 for double-declining). Typical values: 1.5, 2, 2.5.

inputs.label: Total Production Units
inputs.businessContext: Estimated total units to be produced over life. Used in units-of-production method.

inputs.label: Units Produced This Year
inputs.businessContext: Actual units produced in current period for units-of-production method.

inputs.label: Data Confidence
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level in input data (0-100%). Used for adjusted output.

rules: salvageValue must be less than assetCost
if depreciationMethod == 'declining-balance' then decliningBalanceFactor must be >= 1 and <= 3
if depreciationMethod == 'units-of-production' then productionUnits must be > 0 and unitsProducedThisYear must be >= 0

thresholds.salvageValue > 0.5 * assetCost: High salvage value relative to cost; consider impairment test.
thresholds.usefulLife > 30: Very long useful life; verify asset type and condition.
thresholds.decliningBalanceFactor > 2.5: Aggressive depreciation; may not reflect actual usage.

formulas.annualDepreciation: depreciationMethod == 'straight-line' ? depreciableBase / usefulLife : (depreciationMethod == 'declining-balance' ? (bookValue * decliningBalanceFactor / usefulLife) : (depreciationMethod == 'sum-of-years-digits' ? (remainingLife / sumOfYears) * depreciableBase : (depreciationMethod == 'units-of-production' ? (depreciableBase / productionUnits) * unitsProducedThisYear : 0)))

hiddenLossDrivers: if salvageValue > 0.5 * assetCost then 'High salvage value may indicate overestimated residual value'
if usefulLife > 30 then 'Extended useful life may lead to inaccurate depreciation'
if decliningBalanceFactor > 2.5 then 'Aggressive depreciation may distort financials'
suggestedActions: Review salvage value estimation based on market data.
Consider component depreciation for complex assets.
Align useful life with industry benchmarks (e.g., IRS guidelines).
If using declining balance, ensure factor reflects asset usage pattern.

premiumFeatures: Detailed report with charts and amortization schedule
